name: Tadalafil improved SEAR psychosocial score in PDE5-naive ED patients in Porst 2012
statement: In PDE5-inhibitor-naive men with ED, once-daily tadalafil improved ED treatment satisfaction and SEAR psychosocial outcomes versus placebo, while endothelial measures and nocturnal erections did not differ between groups.
population_or_context: Men with erectile dysfunction, mean age 52 years.
quantitative_note: Least-squares mean difference in SEAR total score change from baseline was 11.8 points (95% CI 5.4 to 18.2; P<0.001) versus placebo.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports SEAR total score effect estimate and treatment satisfaction improvement.

caveats: ED treatment population rather than healthy users.
Mean age was older than the primary 30-50 target range.
Psychosocial effects were measured in ED-specific instruments.
population_profile: PDE5-inhibitor-naive men with ED; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily, with possible down-titration to 2.5 mg, for 12 weeks.
indication_context: On-label ED treatment; indirect for off-label well-being claims.
endpoint_measure: SEAR total score and Erectile Dysfunction Inventory of Treatment Satisfaction.
effect_estimate: SEAR total score least-squares mean difference 11.8 points versus placebo, 95% CI 5.4 to 18.2; P<0.001.
safety_findings: Tadalafil was described as well tolerated; adverse events included back pain, headache, and dyspepsia.
directness_to_target_population: Low-to-moderate for tadalafil exposure but low for the healthy target population because participants had ED.
confounding_and_bias_limits: ED-specific instruments and improved erections make it difficult to isolate a direct effect on mood or general well-being.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is ED-linked endpoint specificity rather than agent identity.
